does: This paper introduces Tree of Attacks with Pruning (TAP), an automated black-box jailbreaking method that uses an attacker LLM to iteratively refine candidate prompts toward successfully eliciting harmful outputs from target models. The approach constructs a tree of prompt variations, evaluating and pruning branches unlikely to succeed before querying the target LLM, thereby reducing query costs. TAP achieves jailbreak success rates exceeding 80% against state-of-the-art models including GPT-4 Turbo and GPT-4o, substantially surpassing prior black-box methods while requiring fewer queries to the target system.
connects: This work extends prior research on adversarial prompts and jailbreaking techniques, building on earlier demonstrations that LLMs can be manipulated to bypass safety constraints through carefully crafted inputs. It responds to the challenge of developing scalable, automated attack methods that don't require white-box access to model internals, improving upon previous black-box jailbreaking approaches that typically required larger query budgets or achieved lower success rates. The tree-search formulation and pruning strategy draw on planning and search methodologies in AI, while the use of an attacker LLM reflects the broader pattern of leveraging language models themselves to optimize adversarial attacks.
enables: This work highlights critical vulnerabilities in current LLM safety mechanisms and provides a systematic methodology for identifying them automatically. It enables future research into more robust defense mechanisms by establishing a strong baseline attack that defenders must contend with, potentially spurring development of adversarial training approaches or improved guardrails. The efficient query-based attack framework also opens pathways for studying the transferability of jailbreaks across different models and architectures, and for understanding fundamental limitations in alignment techniques that might be overcome through novel safety approaches.
